## Summary

What does this PR do, in 1–3 sentences? Lead with the user-visible change or the bug being fixed.

## Linked spec or issue

- Spec implemented (if any): `specs/milestone-X-.../NN-name.md` (e.g. `M1-04`)
- Issue closed (if any): closes #
- If neither applies, briefly justify the change.

## Changes

- Bullet list of meaningful changes — one bullet per concern, not one per file.

## Testing

- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] `pytest` passes locally
- [ ] `ruff check` passes
- [ ] `mypy --strict` passes
- [ ] Integration tests run (if applicable):

## Checklist

- [ ] Every commit is signed off (`git commit -s`) — required by the [DCO](../CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-certificate-of-origin-dco)
- [ ] PR title is short and descriptive (under 70 chars)
- [ ] Spec drift, if any, is captured (inline updates by `spec-keeper` or a manual proposal)
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or `.env` files committed
- [ ] No unrelated changes ("while I was in there" cleanups belong in their own PR)

## Notes for reviewers

Anything the reviewer should pay particular attention to — risky areas, deferred follow-ups, decisions made during implementation that diverged from the spec.
